seoTitle: Transfer Funds Between Accounts in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Learn how to transfer funds between accounts in QuickBooks Online to manage cash flow, payroll, and operational expenses accurately.
intro: Moving money between business accounts — for payroll, operating expenses, or reserves — needs to be recorded correctly so both accounts reflect the change. This guide explains how to transfer funds between accounts in QuickBooks Online without creating discrepancies. It's for business owners and finance professionals managing multiple accounts.

steps.heading: Navigate to the transfer feature
steps.body: Open the transfer function within QuickBooks Online, typically found in the banking or plus menu.

steps.heading: Select the source and destination accounts
steps.body: Choose which account the funds are coming from and which account they're going to.

steps.heading: Enter the transfer amount and date
steps.body: Input the exact amount and date of the transfer to keep your records aligned with your actual bank activity.

steps.heading: Avoid common mistakes
steps.body: Double-check you're not duplicating the transfer if it also appears in your bank feed, which can cause reconciliation errors.

steps.heading: Confirm both accounts reflect the change
steps.body: Review both the source and destination account registers to verify the transfer posted correctly on each side.

faq.question: Does transferring funds between accounts count as income or expense?
faq.answer: No, a transfer moves money between your own accounts and doesn't count as income or an expense.

faq.question: What happens if a transfer also appears in my bank feed?
faq.answer: You should match the manually entered transfer with the corresponding bank feed transaction to avoid duplicate entries.

faq.question: Can I automate recurring transfers, like for payroll funding?
faq.answer: Yes, if the transfer happens on a predictable schedule, you can set up a recurring transaction for it.

faq.question: Is this feature useful for businesses with multiple bank accounts?
faq.answer: Yes, it's specifically designed to help track money moving between operating, savings, or payroll accounts accurately.
